_comment: 18-Year Cycle Tripwires — macro crash-factor readings for the Dashboard card (js/tabs/cycle_tripwires.js). Editorial readings + thresholds live here; the user's per-factor status clicks + notes live in localStorage jb_cycle_tripwires. Refresh the live HY spread with: python3 automation/fetch_cycle_tripwires.py. All 'reading' values carry an 'as_of' date per RULE #2 — never present as live-now.
headline: The window the 18-year land cycle points at is open. Land is already rolling over; the financial blow-off is textbook late-cycle. The one missing ingredient is credit stress — spreads waking up is the starting gun.

hy_oas.source: FRED BAMLH0A0HYM2 (ICE BofA US HY OAS)
hy_oas.note: Near 25-year tights. No cycle bust in the record began while credit was this calm. >350bp = stirring; >400bp close = it's starting.

factors.name: Land / housing rollover
factors.plain: Land turns down FIRST in this cycle — before stocks. This is the trigger the theory watches.
factors.reading: Case-Shiller +1.9% y/y (slowest since 2023); pending sales −5.4% in June, steepest drop of the year; ~40–50% of builders cutting prices.

factors.name: Equity valuation (Shiller CAPE)
factors.plain: How stretched stocks are vs a decade of real earnings. A loaded spring, not a trigger — tells you how far the fall could be, not when.
factors.reading: CAPE 41.1 — above 40 for only the 2nd time in 100 years (first was the 2000 dot-com peak).
factors.tripwire: Already extreme — flag stays lit. Watch for equities putting in a LOWER HIGH.

factors.name: Retail leverage (margin debt)
factors.plain: How much borrowed money is holding the market up. Rising fast = the last buyers levering in; the danger is the UNWIND.
factors.reading: $1.42T record, +53.7% y/y (May). Prior clusters of this growth pace: 1999–2000, mid-2007, spring 2021 — all late-cycle.
factors.tripwire: y/y growth rolls over from peak (leverage starts unwinding).

factors.name: HY credit spreads
factors.plain: The single most important gauge here. Junk-bond risk premium. Every big bust began when THIS woke up — not before.
factors.reading: ~269bp — near 25-year tights. Credit sees no stress yet.

factors.name: AI capex / circular financing
factors.plain: The dot-com rhyme. Nvidia→OpenAI→GPU spend loops back to Nvidia; demand looks bigger and more independent than it is.
factors.reading: BIS (Jun 2026) flagged AI-capex bust + circular financing + sovereign debt as the top 3 risks. Hyperscaler capex ~94% of operating cash flow; ~$662B of signed data-center leases sit off balance sheet.
factors.tripwire: First hyperscaler CUTS forward capex guidance, OR a marquee AI-infra debt deal fails to fund.

factors.name: Private credit stress
factors.plain: The shadow-banking pipe. Opaque, marked-to-manager — stress hides until redemptions force it into the open. The amplifier, not the trigger.
factors.reading: PIK interest (paying debt with more debt) jumped 5%→11% of the market; one BDC marked NAV down 19% in a quarter; redemptions exceeding liquidity.
factors.tripwire: A large BDC gates redemptions, or a double-digit NAV markdown hits mainstream headlines.

factors.name: CRE / regional banks
factors.plain: The closest thing to a 2008-style land→bank pipe, relocated from houses to office towers.
factors.reading: $875B of CRE loans mature in 2026; office CMBS delinquency at a record 12.34%; CRE = 44% of regional-bank balance sheets; >54% of regionals exceed the 300% CRE-to-capital line.
factors.tripwire: A regional bank fails or needs rescue over CRE (SVB-style deposit run).
